text.title: Design System Drift Audit Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Design System Drift Audit must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Audit produces a list of findings with severity, evidence link, and named remediator. Findings without evidence links are not findings.

rationale.source: Audit-trail-traceability requirement common to ISO 27001, SOC 2 audits

rule.rationale: Audit-trail-traceability requirement common to ISO 27001, SOC 2 audits.

rule.statement: Sample size is declared upfront and traceable. Sampling method (random, stratified, exhaustive) is recorded with the audit artefact.
Audit cadence is fixed and the previous audit's findings are reviewed at the start of the next. Carry-over open findings cap at N cycles.
rule.rationale: without follow-up the audit loop opens.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Design-system-as-code lifecycle: tokens → Storybook → Figma library → PR → governance Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
